sourceMovie.title: Mirai
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Mirai works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Boy and the Beast
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mirai clicked for you, The Boy and the Beast (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mirai?
faq.answer: Try The Boy and the Beast, Kiki's Delivery Service, Maquia: When the Promised Flower Blooms—each shares DNA with Mirai while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mirai?
faq.answer: Mirai (2018) is best described as Animation, Family, Fantasy, Adventure, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mirai worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
